intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though severely compromised.

inventory_reconciliation.p1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.p2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Present: Pothos specimen with white rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Combined black pot shows only soil and debris; no viable plant tissue.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos shows significant leaf loss and chlorosis. The white rabbit anchor remains, but the plant is in a state of advanced decline.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P3/P4 area).
biome_observations.surface: Desk surface is clear of additional biological growth.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green stem/leaf in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Stem/leaf in black pot shows signs of postural collapse and dehydration.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Total loss of visible green tissue; the biome has transitioned to a state of complete dormancy or death under low-light conditions.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a critical state of failure. The lack of chlorophyll-rich tissue and the collapse of the Pothos specimen suggest severe environmental stress, likely due to insufficient light and potential desiccation.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments suggests an undocumented soil amendment intervention. The transition to near-total darkness in the final frames suggests a failure of the fixed lighting system.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic decline in the indoor biome. Starting from a state of minimal viability, the specimens have progressively withered. The Pothos (P3) has lost its structural integrity, and the other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively barren. The environment is currently hostile to plant life, characterized by darkness and likely nutrient/water depletion.
